name: Swiss Steak (3 Versions, Stove Top, Crockpot, Oven)
recipeIngredient: 1 pound boneless beef round steak, cut into 3/4-inch thick slices
1 small onion, sliced and separated into rings
2 cups cooked hot noodles or rice
recipeInstructions:
(STOVE TOP VERSION):
Trim excess fat from the steak. Cut into 4 serving pieces.
Combine flour, salt, and pepper in a shallow dish.
Using the notched side of a meat mallet, pound each piece to an even thickness.
In a large skillet, heat oil over medium-high heat. Brown the steak on both sides until golden brown, about 2-3 minutes per side. Remove from the skillet and set aside.
Reduce heat to medium. In the same skillet, sauté onion, celery, and carrot for 5 minutes, or until softened.
Add tomatoes, thyme, salt, and pepper. Simmer for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Cover and simmer for 1 hour 15 minutes, or until meat is tender. Skim off any foam.
Serve with noodles or rice. Makes 4 servings.
(CROCKPOT VERSION):
Trim excess fat from the steak. Cut into 4 serving pieces.
In a slow cooker, combine onion, celery, carrot, thyme, salt, pepper, and 2 tablespoons quick-cooking tapioca.
Pour undrained tomatoes over vegetables. Add meat on top.
Cover and cook on low for 10-12 hours. Serve with noodles or rice.
(OVEN VERSION):
Prepare the steak as directed in the stove top version.
Transfer to a 2-quart baking dish.
In the same skillet, combine tomatoes, onion, celery, carrot, thyme, salt, and pepper. Bring to boil, scraping up any browned bits.
Pour over meat. Cover with foil.
Bake at 350°F for 1 hour or until tender. Serve with noodles or rice.
